Title: Integrated omics reveal novel functions and underlying mechanisms of the receptor kinase FERONIA in Arabidopsis thaliana

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream negative regulator whose loss increases indole glucosinolate accumulation and biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulation partly through transcriptional control via NAI1; phosphorylation-dependent control of downstream regulators
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses NAI1 and thereby reduces expression of IG biosynthetic genes such as ASA1, IGMT5, MYC2, CYP83B1, CYP79B2, and CYP79B3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores and other biotic stresses
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied in 10-day-old seedlings, 3-week-old rosette leaves, and 4.5-week-old rosettes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (fer-4); complementation with FERpro:FER-GFP and kinase-dead FERpro:FERK565R-GFP; RT-qPCR; glucosinolate metabolite profiling by HPLC
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day growth conditions; ABA treatment for ABI5-related assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: fer mutants show pronounced stunted growth and ABA hypersensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune glucosinolate levels and defense metabolism, but manipulation may cause growth penalties because FER broadly regulates growth and stress pathways
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of FER increased transcript and/or protein levels of many indole glucosinolate biosynthetic genes. 2. fer mutants accumulated higher I3M, 4MOI3M, and 1MOI3M. 3. NAI1 acts downstream of FER and is required for full elevation of I3M and 4MOI3M in fer. 4. FER kinase activity is important for repression of IG biosynthetic gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FER is the main focus of the paper and also regulates ER body formation and ABA responses; for nutrient-metabolism extraction, the supported metabolic role is as an upstream negative regulator of indole glucosinolate biosynthesis, partially through NAI1.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes indole glucosinolate biosynthesis downstream of FER, contributing to elevated I3M and 4MOI3M
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required for full accumulation of specific indole glucosinolates in the fer background
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports expression of MYC2 and ER body-associated genes; indirectly promotes IG biosynthetic output
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ER body-associated defense metabolism and partial control of indole glucosinolate biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in fer mutant rosette leaves and seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic analysis using nai1 and fer nai1 double mutant; RT-qPCR; glucosinolate metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to modulate ER body-linked indole glucosinolate traits, but effects are partial and coupled to ER body developmental programs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: 3'-RNA-seq (GEO: GSE143634; GSE191303); proteomics/phosphoproteomics context from same study (MassIVE: MSV000084804)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAI1 transcript is elevated in fer. 2. Disruption of NAI1 in fer lowers I3M and 4MOI3M relative to fer. 3. NAI1 is therefore a downstream positive regulator mediating part of FER control over indole glucosinolate biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NAI1 did not affect 1MOI3M in this study, consistent with independent regulation of that IG species.
